name: Greece
summary: Greece combines Mediterranean warmth with altitude, wind and volcanic soils, producing wines with high acidity and distinctive character.

santorini.name: Santorini
santorini.styleSummary: Very high-acid whites despite hot climate, with mineral and saline character.

Assyrtiko.style: Citrus, mineral, saline, very high acidity.
viticulture: Trained low to ground to protect from wind

naoussa.name: Naoussa
naoussa.styleSummary: Structured, ageworthy reds with high tannin and acidity.

Xinomavro.style: Red fruit, tomato leaf, high acidity, high tannin.

nemea.name: Nemea
nemea.styleSummary: Ripe, fruit-driven reds with varying structure depending on altitude.

Agiorgitiko.style: Ripe red fruit, medium tannin, softer style than Xinomavro.

mantinia.name: Mantinia
mantinia.styleSummary: Aromatic whites with high acidity and fresh style.

Moschofilero.style: Floral, citrus, aromatic with high acidity.

samos.name: Samos
samos.styleSummary: Sweet wines from Muscat with intense aromatics.

Muscat.style: Floral, grapey, sweet with high aroma intensity.
